tools.name: phone_validate_number
tools.description:
Use this when you need to validate a phone number and get carrier info. Returns structured validation data in JSON.

Returns: 1. valid (boolean) 2. e164 format string 3. countryCode and countryName 4. carrier name 5. lineType (mobile/landline/voip/toll-free) 6. localFormat and internationalFormat.

tools.name: phone_validate_batch
tools.description:
Use this when you need to validate multiple phone numbers at once (up to 50). Returns an array of validation results in JSON.

Returns per number: 1. valid (boolean) 2. e164 format 3. countryCode and countryName 4. carrier name 5. lineType (mobile/landline/voip/toll-free).

Example output: {"results

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: phone_validate_number · Private data: phone_validate_batch · Public sink: phone_validate_number, phone_validate_batch · Destructive: phone_validate_number, phone_validate_batch.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: phone_validate_number · Private data: phone_validate_batch · Public sink: phone_validate_number, phone_validate_batch · Destructive: phone_validate_number, phone_validate_batch

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
